items.title: June 20, 1993: Scottie Pippen drives and dunks on Charles Barkley in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 20, 1993: Scottie Pippen drives and dunks on Charles Barkley in Game 6 of the NBA Finals.

Pippen had 23 PTS/12 REB/5 AST/4 STL and Michael Jordan had 33 PTS/8 REB/7 AST in the Bulls' 99-98 win over the Phoenix Suns that earned Chicago its third-straight NBA Championship.

items.title: Another offensive showcase in the first half, Fever leading 59-56. Shooting 61%, including 9 for 15 from deep.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Another offensive showcase in the first half, Fever leading 59-56. Shooting 61%, including 9 for 15 from deep.

Mitchell has 16pts, Clark with 15.  Dream with 12-0 adv. in second-chance points.

It's also been physical, full of fouls. Nine of 10 starters have 2+ fouls.

items.title: Bonus Hollander POTD Derrick Coleman. Syracuse PF was the 1st pick by NJ in 90. Was really good first 4 yrs (20 ppg, 10.6 rpg) won 90-91 ROY & all-star in 93-94. Was poster child for Gen X complaints by old timers. Traded to PHI in 95, was role guy there & in CHA. Retired in 05.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Bonus Hollander POTD Derrick Coleman. Syracuse PF was the 1st pick by NJ in 90. Was really good first 4 yrs (20 ppg, 10.6 rpg) won 90-91 ROY & all-star in 93-94. Was poster child for Gen X complaints by old timers. Traded to PHI in 95, was role guy there & in CHA. Retired in 05.

items.title: This is interesting because, well, he is still in his prime, a 2x MVP, a top 25 player in the history of the sport and immediately makes you a contender.
items.author: Jim Owczarski
items.body_excerpt:
This is interesting because, well, he is still in his prime, a 2x MVP, a top 25 player in the history of the sport and immediately makes you a contender.

Why would/should it be easy to get him? 

The Bucks aren't folding. They still have to field a team...

items.title: If the Wolves do something big this summer, they're going to have to do it via trade. I wouldn't rule that out, but it's going to cost them rotation players. Beyond that, re-signing Dosunmu and others for depth is key.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
If the Wolves do something big this summer, they're going to have to do it via trade. I wouldn't rule that out, but it's going to cost them rotation players. Beyond that, re-signing Dosunmu and others for depth is key.

More about Minnesota's summer ⬇️
